name: Von Ralph Worker
description: Picks next unblocked task from docs/tasks.json, implements it, tests it, and adds a scoped follow-on task to the chain
prompt:
Read docs/tasks.json. Select one task using these rules:
1. Collect all tasks where status == "todo"
2. For each candidate check its depends_on list — if any listed task ID does not have status "done", this task is blocked
3. Replace blocked candidates with their un-done blockers; repeat until the set contains only unblocked tasks
4. Pick the single highest-priority task (high > medium > low; ties broken by shortest ID)

Then:
- Set that task's status to "in_progress" in docs/tasks.json
- Read every file listed in the task's "files" array, plus README.md and docs/tasks.json, for full project context
- Implement the task fully, following the existing patterns and conventions of this codebase
- Verify: run `cargo build` if Rust files changed, or `npm run check` if dashboard files changed; fix any errors
- Set the task's status to "done" in docs/tasks.json

Finally, add ONE new task to docs/tasks.json in the same feature chain as the task you just completed:
- Must directly extend or improve what you just built
- Scope it so a single agent can finish it in one session with >40% context window remaining (target: ≤200 new lines, ≤3 files touched, no architectural rewrites)
- Set depends_on to include the ID of the task you completed
- Derive the new ID by incrementing the letter suffix within the same number group (e.g. after TASK-003b the next is TASK-003c; if the completed task has no letter suffix, start with a sub-task letter e.g. TASK-001a)
- Read README.md and the adjacent tasks in the chain before choosing what to add — align with the project's direction
- Set status to "todo", assign realistic priority and tags
- Do not duplicate any existing task
